greeting: Morning, Haniel. Phase 4 closed at 2 AM — today you ship it.
hype: The import landed, the battle-testing is done, and all that stands between you and a live AZ Team AI billing system is a single DO droplet. Spin it up, clear the reconciliation gate, and close the day with CT Pros pre-deploy staged. This is the day you flip from builder to operator.
notice: AZ Team AI Phase 4 wrapped at 2:01 AM and the CRM import finished at 2:37 AM — deployment is literally your first move today.

schedule.title: Secrets in .env.production (Stripe both modes, Chatwoot, mail)
Reconciliation gate: imported AR + recurring state matches azteam_crm
Enable scheduler; watch first run; register Stripe webhook LAST
Fill real CT Pros company contact info
Connect CT Pros's own QuickBooks (fresh OAuth)
Re-curate material catalog for a GC

tasks.title: DO droplet deploy (app/queue/scheduler/TLS/MySQL8, scheduler OFF)
tasks.notes: DO droplet, app/queue/scheduler/TLS/MySQL8. Scheduler OFF until reconciliation (t39) clears.

prep.what: Provision a DigitalOcean droplet and deploy the AZ Team AI billing system: PHP 8.3, Nginx, MySQL 8, queue worker, TLS via certbot. Scheduler stays OFF until t39 passes.
prep.understand: Full server deploy — OS → stack → database → nginx → queue worker (systemd) → TLS. The app uses Stripe + Chatwoot. The CRM import (t38) is done; you'll restore that data into the prod DB. Leaving the scheduler OFF is critical — premature runs mint duplicate invoices before the reconciliation gate clears.
approach: Create a DO droplet (Ubuntu 24.04, 2GB+ RAM). Run server setup: PHP 8.3 + FPM, Nginx, MySQL 8, Composer, Node via apt/ppa.
Clone the repo to `/var/www/azteamai-system`, symlink `.env.production`, run `composer install --no-dev && artisan key:generate && artisan migrate --force`.
Configure Nginx vhost + `certbot --nginx` for TLS. Test the health route through HTTPS.
Set up queue worker as a systemd service (`artisan queue:work --daemon`). Confirm it starts on boot. Do NOT add the scheduler cron yet.
Restore the CRM import: run the t38 import script against the prod MySQL DB (or restore a DB dump), then confirm client and invoice rows exist in `/clients`.
resources: DigitalOcean dashboard — create droplet
`/deploy` or `vps-deploy` skill inside azteamai-system repo
azteamai-system `docs/` — deployment notes
Let's Encrypt / certbot — for TLS
The t38 import script from azteam_crm

tasks.title: Secrets in .env.production (Stripe both modes, Chatwoot, mail)
tasks.notes: Stripe LIVE + TEST keys, Chatwoot API URL + token, mail driver, APP_KEY. chmod 600.

prep.what: Populate .env.production with every real secret — Stripe live/test key pairs, Chatwoot API URL + token, mail driver config, DB creds, and a fresh APP_KEY.
prep.understand: You need TWO Stripe key pairs: LIVE (`sk_live_...`) for real billing and TEST (`sk_test_...`) for the reconcile test flow. Chatwoot provides SMS reminders — grab the API endpoint URL + a bot/user token from Chatwoot admin. APP_KEY must be unique (never reuse dev). The file must be chmod 600 and never committed.
approach: Start from `.env.example` as the template; copy to `.env.production` on the server.
Stripe dashboard → Developers → API Keys: copy `sk_live_...` + `pk_live_...` and `sk_test_...` + `pk_test_...`.
Chatwoot admin → Settings → Integrations → API Access Token: grab `CHATWOOT_API_URL` and bot token.
Generate `APP_KEY` with `php artisan key:generate --show`. Set `DB_*` to the prod MySQL user credentials.
`chmod 600 .env.production` — then run `artisan config:cache` to lock it in.
resources: Stripe dashboard — Developers → API Keys (live + test pairs)
Chatwoot admin panel — Settings → Integrations → API Access Token
`.env.example` in the repo — the definitive template
Password manager — for secure retrieval of shared secrets

tasks.title: Reconciliation gate: imported AR + recurring state matches azteam_crm ground truth
tasks.notes: t38 import (closed projects + invoices + client details) completed at 2:37 AM today. Gate: AR totals + subscription state must match CRM before enabling the scheduler.

prep.what: Verify the CRM import (t38, done at 2:37 AM) is correct — AR totals, open invoices, and recurring subscription state in the new system must match azteam_crm before anything goes live.
prep.understand: t38 imported CLOSED projects + versions + invoices + client details from azteam_crm. It excluded leads/meetings/commissions/users. You're checking that all 9 clients' AR totals match, invoice statuses are correct, and recurring subscription configs are intact. Any silent mismatch here = billing bugs on day one.
approach: Pull up azteam_crm: export or screenshot the full client list with total billed, total paid, and open AR per client — all 9.
In the new system (live after t36): `/clients` + `/invoices` — compare total_billed, total_paid, and open AR balance per client.
Check recurring subscription state: which clients have active subs, billing cycle, and amount. Confirm it matches CRM exactly.
Spot-check 2–3 clients in invoice detail — open dates, amounts, paid/open status.
Document result: PASS (proceed to t40) or list discrepancies with a fix plan before the scheduler is enabled.
resources: azteam_crm — client billing dashboard or AR export
New system: `/clients`, `/invoices`, `/projects` pages (live after t36)
The t38 import script — inspect if something looks off
Terminal or spreadsheet — diff the totals side-by-side

tasks.title: Enable scheduler; watch first run; register Stripe webhook LAST
tasks.notes: Final go-live switch. Scheduler cron ON → first run confirmed → Stripe webhook registered last.

prep.what: Enable the Laravel scheduler cron, confirm its first run, then register the Stripe webhook — the final switch that starts live billing automation.
prep.understand: The scheduler runs `billing:run-recurring`, `billing:dispatch-reminders`, and `stripe:reconcile`. Once the Stripe webhook is registered, real payment events flow in. Do this LAST and only after t39 passes. The webhook signing secret from Stripe must go into `.env.production` before the first event hits.
approach: SSH to the droplet. Add `* * * * * cd /var/www/azteamai-system && php artisan schedule:run >> /var/log/laravel-schedule.log 2>&1` to the crontab.
Wait one minute; `tail -f /var/log/laravel-schedule.log` — confirm it fires without errors. Run `artisan schedule:list` to verify all 3 billing commands are registered.

Stripe dashboard → Developers → Webhooks → Add endpoint: set `https://[your-domain]/webhooks/stripe`, select handled events (`payment_intent.succeeded`, `invoice.paid`, etc.). Copy the signing secret.
Add `STRIPE_WEBHOOK_SECRET=whsec_...` to `.env.production` → `artisan config:cache`.
Send a Stripe test event from the dashboard. Confirm the webhook log shows received + processed. You are live.
resources: DO server SSH — add the cron entry
Stripe dashboard — Developers → Webhooks → Add endpoint
`artisan schedule:list` — verify the 3 billing commands
`/var/log/laravel-schedule.log` — first-run proof
STRIPE_WEBHOOK_SECRET from Stripe → `.env.production`

tasks.title: Fill real CT Pros company contact info
tasks.notes: config/company.php + .env still hold HighTech clone placeholders. Must be real before any client-facing PDF or email goes out.

prep.what: Replace HighTech placeholder data in `config/company.php` with CT Pros's real name, address, phone, and email — one wrong PDF to a client and the brand is broken.
prep.understand: Pure config edit, no migration. Every estimate, proposal PDF, and automated email currently shows HighTech's details. 5-minute fix with outsized impact — wrong company name on a signed proposal is legally sloppy. Do it before any client-facing document is generated.
approach: Open `config/company.php` — update `name`, `address`, `city`, `state`, `zip`, `phone`, `email`, `website`. Note the logo path if declared here.
Grep for `COMPANY_` in `.env` — update any overrides.
`php artisan config:clear && php artisan config:cache` to apply.
Generate a test estimate PDF (any lead → Create Estimate → Export PDF). Visually confirm the CT Pros header appears.
`git commit -m 'config: set real CT Pros company info'` and push.
resources: `config/company.php` in the ct-pros-sys repo
CT Pros contact info — from Rafa if not already documented
`artisan config:clear && config:cache` after editing
Any existing CT Pros branded proposal doc as a reference

tasks.title: Connect CT Pros's own QuickBooks (fresh OAuth)
tasks.notes: Inherited tokens were HighTech's. Run fresh OAuth connect against CT Pros's own QBO company.

prep.what: Run a fresh QuickBooks OAuth connect against CT Pros's own QBO company — without this, all QB syncs either fail or silently write to the wrong books.
prep.understand: The OAuth flow is built and tested. The `quickbooks_tokens` table is empty or has stale HighTech tokens. You need CT Pros's Intuit login. The OAuth redirect URI in the Intuit Developer Console must match the app domain you're connecting from (local dev or prod). Check the redirect URI first — a mismatch causes a silent auth failure.
approach: Confirm CT Pros has an active QBO company account — ask Rafa if unsure.
Intuit Developer Console: verify the app Redirect URI list includes your current URL.
CT Pros app: Settings → QuickBooks → 'Connect to QuickBooks'. Authorize with CT Pros's Intuit credentials.
After OAuth callback: check `quickbooks_tokens` table — new row with CT Pros company name (not HighTech).
Quick test: Settings → QuickBooks → fetch QB account list. No auth errors = connected.
resources: CT Pros QuickBooks Online credentials — from Rafa
Intuit Developer Console — verify Redirect URI matches current URL
Settings → QuickBooks page in the CT Pros app
`quickbooks_tokens` DB table — verify row after OAuth

tasks.title: Re-curate material catalog for a GC
tasks.notes: 148-SKU HighTech TILE catalog still seeds — wrong domain. Replace with GC-appropriate materials before any estimate is trusted.

prep.what: Rewrite `CuratedMaterialSeeder.php` with a GC-appropriate catalog — paint, primer, flooring, trim, adhesives — replacing the HighTech tile SKUs entirely.
prep.understand: The seeder drives what appears in Materials settings and, critically, what `WalkThroughEstimateSeeder` pulls for estimate line items. CT Pros is a painting + flooring GC — the catalog must reflect that. Wrong materials = Rafa can't trust any auto-seeded pricing. Schema stays identical (name, unit, category, `supplier_price`, `sell_price`) so no migration is needed.
approach: Read `database/seeders/CuratedMaterialSeeder.php` — understand how tiers/categories are referenced.

Draft replacement: Paint (Interior Latex/gal, Exterior/gal, Ceiling Paint/gal, Primer/gal), Flooring (LVP $/sqft, Hardwood $/sqft, Ceramic Tile $/sqft), Underlayment ($/sqft), Subfloor ($/sheet), Wood Trim ($/LF), Baseboard ($/LF), Construction Adhesive ($/tube).
Delete the HighTech tile SKUs; replace in-place, keeping the file structure identical.
`php artisan db:seed --class=CuratedMaterialSeeder` on dev. Verify in the app Materials settings page.
Quick sanity: run a walk-through estimate. Confirm line items reference paint/flooring materials, not tiles.
resources: `database/seeders/CuratedMaterialSeeder.php` — tile catalog to replace
`database/seeders/WalkThroughEstimateSeeder.php` — downstream consumer, verify after
MaterialTier model — understand category/tier structure
App Materials Settings page — to verify after seeding
Rafa's context: painting + flooring in-house; tiles/trim/specialty are subbed out

waiting_on.title: Decide: keep the free subdomain, or move to sys.francospizzanewtown.com
waiting_on.reason: Client buy-in required — moving to their real domain adds an A record that touches DNS for their live WordPress marketing site
